personas.name: Budget-Conscious Free User
personas.description: A person comparing meditation apps who wants Medito to stay genuinely free, usable without ads, and optional about donations.
goals: Try meditation content before deciding whether to donate
Use a meditation app that still feels complete without paying
pain_points: Donation prompts can feel too close to a paywall
Many competing apps lock content behind subscriptions
Users are skeptical when an app claims to be free but asks for money too early
context: Comparing Medito with paid meditation apps
Looking for low-cost mental wellness support
Open to donating later if the app proves useful
constraints: Core content must stay available without payment
Donation requests should be dismissible and should not block playback

personas.name: Bedtime Listener
personas.description: A person who opens Medito at night and depends on sleep stories, sleep meditations, and calm playback to fall asleep or get back to sleep.
goals: Keep bedtime audio playing without interruption
Find sleep content with very little effort late at night
pain_points: Short stories may end before the listener falls asleep
Interruptions between tracks break the bedtime routine
Sleep content is frustrating when it takes too many taps to reach
context: Using the app in bed, often with the screen dimmed or off
Returning to the app after waking during the night
Switching between sleep stories, meditations, and calming sounds
constraints: Bedtime playback should avoid blocking popups
Sleep content should be grouped clearly inside the app

personas.name: Emotional Support Seeker
personas.description: A person turning to Medito during anxiety, stress, panic, grief, or emotional overload and needing focused support without extra searching.
goals: Find sessions that match the current emotional state
Return quickly to sessions that have helped before
pain_points: It is harder to browse calmly when someone is already distressed
Specific support topics can be buried in a large library
Helpful sessions are easy to lose if they cannot be saved or reopened quickly
context: Using the app during panic, grief, anxiety, or high stress
Sometimes relying on meditation for immediate emotional regulation
Looking for structured support rather than a random session list
constraints: Support content should be grouped by need
Previously helpful sessions should be easy to reopen from the home screen

personas.name: Habit-Building Beginner
personas.description: A beginner or returning meditator who uses Medito to learn step by step and stay consistent through courses, reminders, streaks, and visible progress.
goals: Learn meditation in a guided order
See enough progress to stay motivated
pain_points: Beginners can lose momentum when the next step is unclear
Broken streaks or missing progress reduce motivation
A habit is harder to maintain without reminders
context: Returning to the app daily or trying to restart a routine
Tracking streaks, total sessions, or lesson progress
constraints: The course path should show what comes next
Reminder settings and progress tracking should stay reliable

personas.name: Reliability-Focused Regular User
personas.description: A frequent Medito user who expects playback, core features, and saved progress to keep working across updates, sessions, and devices.
goals: Start and finish sessions without crashes or audio failures
Keep using the app after updates without losing key functions
See progress and streak data follow the account across devices
pain_points: Updates can break search, downloads, or playback
Audio may stop, freeze, or fail after the screen is off
Progress and streak data can disappear or fail to sync
context: Reopening the app after updates or on a second device
Depending on the app during daily routines rather than one-off use
constraints: Playback must remain stable during normal listening conditions
Signed-in progress should be restored after sign-in and sync between devices
